name: the-road-to-serfdom
description: F. A. Hayek's The Road to Serfdom — classical liberal toolkit explaining why centralized planning leads to totalitarianism and why the rule of law is essential to freedom
useCase: Understand the case against central planning, defend the rule of law, recognize the mechanisms that turn intervention into tyranny, and revive the classical liberal tradition
knownRisks: Hayek's slippery slope argument can be used to oppose all government intervention, even policies that are compatible with freedom (universal healthcare, basic safety nets)
The book was written in 1944 — some specific examples are dated, even if the principles remain relevant
Hayek's distinction between law and command is sharp in theory but blurry in practice — many policies lie in a grey zone
